howItWorks.intro: This calories burned calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Individual results vary based on metabolism, genetics, and activity level. Consult your doctor before starting any program.

faq.question: What is MET?
faq.answer: Metabolic Equivalent of Task. MET 1 = sitting still. Walking = 3-4 MET, cycling = 6-8, running = 8-12, swimming = 6-10. Higher MET = more intense.

faq.question: How accurate is this?
faq.answer: MET-based estimates are within 15-20% for most people. Actual burn depends on intensity, fitness level, body composition, and environmental factors.

faq.question: How many calories to lose 1 pound?
faq.answer: Roughly 3,500 calories equals 1 pound of fat. A 500 cal/day deficit through diet and exercise produces about 1 lb/week loss.

faq.question: Does exercise after-burn count?
faq.answer: Yes — EPOC (excess post-exercise oxygen consumption) can burn 6-15% additional calories after intense exercise. This calculator estimates direct burn only.

howWeCalculate.intro: This calories burned calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.

whenToUse.intro: This calories burned calculator is designed for anyone who needs quick, reliable calculations without complex spreadsheets or professional consultations.
